# Tasks — Template Helpers Option
*Generated by create_tasks on 2026-03-19*

## Objective
Support a `helpers:` option in `use Juvet.Template` so helper module functions are automatically available as bindings in templates via dot-call syntax.

## Scope
- **In Scope**: Accept and store `:helpers` option in `__using__/1`, generate function captures at compile time, merge into bindings in all generated function paths, compile-time conflict detection, tests
- **Out of Scope**: Tatsu-side changes, runtime helper loading, helper module validation beyond conflict detection, changes to tokenizer/parser/compiler

## Requirements Traced
| ID | Description | Source | Tasks |
|----|-------------|--------|-------|
| REQ-001 | `use Juvet.Template, helpers: [Module]` stores helper modules | user spec | 1.1 |
| REQ-002 | Helper module public functions become bindings as function captures | user spec | 1.2 |
| REQ-003 | User bindings take precedence over helper bindings | user spec | 1.2 |
| REQ-004 | Compile-time error on conflicting function names across helpers | user spec | 1.2 |
| REQ-005 | Multiple arities: pick highest arity per function name | user spec | 1.2 |

---

## Architecture Context

### Where This Fits
- Extends `__using__/1` in `lib/juvet/template.ex` (line 72) to accept a new option
- Modifies `generate_map_function` (line 635) and `generate_json_function` (line 604) to merge helper captures into bindings before evaluation
- Follows the existing pattern of `format:` option storage via module attributes

### Technical Approach
- Store helpers as `@juvet_template_helpers` module attribute (same pattern as `@juvet_template_format`)
- At compile time in `generate_*_function`, read the helpers attribute, iterate each module's `__info__(:functions)`, generate `&Module.function/arity` captures as a keyword list
- Merge via `Keyword.merge(helpers, bindings)` so user bindings win
- For multiple arities of same function name: keep highest arity (simplest MVP)

### Key Decisions
- Function captures use dot-call syntax in templates: `<%= format_location.(location, :slack) %>`
- Highest arity wins when a helper exports `foo/1` and `foo/2` — the `/2` capture is used
- Conflicts across different helper modules raise `CompileError` at compile time

---

## Tasks

### Phase 1: Implementation

#### [1.1] Accept and store `:helpers` option in `__using__/1`
- [ ] **1.1.1** Modify `__using__/1` in `lib/juvet/template.ex` to extract `:helpers` from opts and store as module attribute
  - **Produces**: `@juvet_template_helpers` module attribute containing list of helper modules (default `[]`)
  - **Consumed by**: `generate_map_function` and `generate_json_function` (task 1.2)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new option, extends existing pattern)
  - [ ] `Keyword.get(opts, :helpers, [])` extracts the helpers list
  - [ ] Stored via `Module.put_attribute` alongside existing `@juvet_template_format`
  - [ ] Default is empty list when no `:helpers` option provided

#### [1.2] Generate helper captures and merge into bindings in all function generation paths
- [ ] **1.2.1** Create a private function that builds the helpers keyword list from helper modules at compile time
  - **Produces**: Keyword list of `[function_name: &Module.function/arity]` captures
  - **Consumed by**: All 6 code paths in `generate_map_function` and `generate_json_function`
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Iterates each helper module's `__info__(:functions)` to get exported functions with arities
  - [ ] For duplicate function names within a module (multiple arities), keeps the highest arity
  - [ ] Raises `CompileError` if two different helper modules export the same function name

- [ ] **1.2.2** Modify all code paths in `generate_map_function` and `generate_json_function` to merge helpers into bindings
  - **Produces**: Generated template functions that include helper captures in bindings
  - **Consumed by**: Template callers at runtime — helpers available via dot-call syntax
  - **Replaces**: N/A (extends existing bindings parameter)
  - [ ] All 3 paths in `generate_map_function` (for-loop/code-block, EEx, static) merge helpers before evaluation
  - [ ] All 3 paths in `generate_json_function` merge helpers before evaluation
  - [ ] User-provided bindings take precedence via `Keyword.merge(helpers, bindings)`
  - [ ] Static path (no EEx) still ignores bindings but the function signature accepts them

### Phase 2: Testing

#### [2.1] Add tests for template helpers
- [ ] **2.1.1** Add tests in `test/juvet/template_test.exs`
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for helpers feature
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test: Template with helper module — helper function available as binding via dot-call
  - [ ] Test: User binding overrides helper binding with same name
  - [ ] Test: Multiple helper modules — functions from all modules available
  - [ ] Test: Conflicting function names across helpers raises compile-time error
  - [ ] Test: Template without helpers option still works (regression)

## Execution Strategies

### Sequential Execution
1. Task 1.1 - Accept and store helpers option (no dependencies)
2. Task 1.2 - Generate captures and merge into bindings (depends on 1.1)
3. Task 2.1 - Tests (depends on 1.1, 1.2)

### Parallel Execution

**Wave 1**: 1.1
- Rationale: Foundation — stores the option

**Wave 2 (after Wave 1)**: 1.2
- Rationale: Needs the stored attribute to read from

**Wave 3 (after Wave 2)**: 2.1
- Rationale: Tests verify the implementation
